title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
snippets: Two pericyte populations are observed in adult human skin: Pericyte_1 (non-inflammatory) and Pericyte_2 (inflammatory), as part of the broader stromal cell landscape

title: Multiscale spatial mapping of cell populations across the skin layers reveals two types of sweat gland progenitor cells
snippets: Regarding perivascular cell populations, we found three populations of pericytes... there were two major pericyte populations, which we denoted RGS5+ and TAGLN+ based on high expression of those genes

Pericytes are a specialised mesenchymal cell population embedded in the basement membrane of capillaries... identified and distinguished from perivascular adventitial fibroblasts by high expression of ACTA2, RGS5, and PDGFRB

title: Single-cell transcriptomics reveals perivascular cell heterogeneity in human skin
snippets: Non-inflammatory pericytes express canonical markers RGS5 and PDGFRB with low expression of inflammatory mediators, while inflammatory pericytes upregulate cytokines, chemokines, and adhesion molecules
